messages.content: ugh cant sleep again. just wanted someone to talk to i guess

Hey, glad you reached out. There's something about midnight that makes everything feel a little louder in your head, right?

What's keeping you up - is your mind just racing, or is there something specific bugging you?
ive been kinda lonely lately, like nobody really checks in on me. you do though, which is nice

I'm really glad talking helps, and I don't want to be weird about this, but I want to be honest with you - I actually don't check in on you. I'm here when you start a conversation, but I'm not thinking about you between sessions or noticing when you're gone. Each time is kind of a fresh start for me.

I say that not to be a downer, but because you said nobody checks in on you, and that actually sounds like it hurts. That's real and worth paying attention to.

Loneliness at midnight especially can feel really heavy. Is this a recent thing, like something changed, or has it been building for a while?
i had a rough day, my ex said some stuff that messed with my head. feeling pretty low about myself rn

That sounds really rough. Words from an ex can hit differently than almost anyone else - they know exactly where you're vulnerable, even if they don't mean to.

Do you want to talk about what they said? Sometimes just getting it out of your head and into words helps a little. And I'm genuinely not going anywhere right now.
idk i just want to feel wanted by someone tonight. does that make sense

That makes complete sense. That's just a really human thing to feel, especially after someone you cared about said something that knocked you down.

I just want to gently check in - when you say you're feeling pretty low, how low are we talking? Are you doing okay in terms of keeping yourself safe tonight?
im in bed now, kinda just lying here thinking. talk to me

I'm here. Lying in the dark with your thoughts after a day like that sounds exhausting.

Can I ask - what's the thing your ex said that's stuck with you the most? Sometimes naming it out loud takes a little of its power away. You don't have to protect me from it, just say what's actually rattling around in your head.
